title: rubus viridis subsp. preissmannii
intro: Rubus viridis subsp. preissmannii, also known as the green raspberry or wild raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus viridis plant, which is native to Western Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Rubus viridis subsp. preissmannii include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to support overall health and well-being.
